title: hyacinthus orientalis subsp. provincialis
intro: Hyacinthus orientalis subsp. provincialis, also known as the Provençal hyacinth, is a subspecies of the eastern squill, a type of flowering bulbous plant native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Provençal hyacinth include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or topical cream, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
